verified_title: EspH, a new cytoskeleton-modulating effector of enterohaemorrhagic and enteropathogenic Escherichia coli
note: Tu 2003 Mol Microbiol; EspH shown translocated by T3SS into infected cells (LEE orf18). Primary EspH characterization. Verified Crossref + PubMed PMID 12535063.

verified_title: Co-ordinate regulation of distinct host cell signalling pathways by multifunctional enteropathogenic Escherichia coli effector molecules
note: Kenny 2002 Mol Microbiol; characterizes Map (mitochondrial-associated protein) as T3SS effector inducing filopodia. PMID 12046591. Matches suggested_fix (Kenny 2002).

verified_title: Proteolytic elimination of N-myristoyl modifications by the Shigella virulence factor IpaJ
note: Burnaevskiy 2013 Nature; IpaJ identified as S. flexneri T3SS effector cysteine protease cleaving N-myristoyl glycine. PMID 23535599. Matches suggested_fix.

verified_title: Microtubule-Severing Activity of Shigella Is Pivotal for Intercellular Spreading
note: Yoshida 2006 Science; VirA secreted via T3SS, alpha-tubulin cysteine-protease microtubule destruction; virA mutant fails to spread. PMID 17095701. Matches suggested_fix (Yoshida 2006 Science). Earlier Yoshida 2002 EMBO J (10.1093/emboj/cdf319) also valid VirA/T3SS primary.

verified_title: EspH, a new cytoskeleton-modulating effector of enterohaemorrhagic and enteropathogenic Escherichia coli
note: Same EspH primary as R083 (Tu 2003). EPEC E2348/69 EspH is the same LEE effector. PMID 12535063.

verified_title: SepZ/EspZ Is Secreted and Translocated into HeLa Cells by the Enteropathogenic Escherichia coli Type III Secretion System
note: Kanack 2005 Infect Immun; EspZ-CyaA fusion translocated into HeLa in T3SS-dependent manner (direct translocation assay). PMID 15972527. Matches suggested_fix (Kanack 2005).

verified_title: Co-ordinate regulation of distinct host cell signalling pathways by multifunctional enteropathogenic Escherichia coli effector molecules
note: Same Map primary as R085 (Kenny 2002). EPEC E2348/69 Map is the same LEE effector. PMID 12046591.
